description: Add stress tests for cross-system chaos: MCP client + side panel acting simultaneously, CLI config changes during active dispatch, multiple MCP sessions competing, and the full system under combined load. These are the highest-value tests because they exercise the real-world scenario where an AI agent, a user, and the CLI are all active at the same time.

userStories.title: MCP client tool call while side panel toggles permission
userStories.description: MCP client calls a tool while the side panel user simultaneously changes that tool's permission. Verify no crash, clean error or success, and final state is consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: New test file e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts exists
Test opens side panel + creates MCP client, both connected to same server
MCP client starts a slow tool call while side panel toggles the tool's permission Off→Auto→Off
The tool call resolves (either success or clean error)
Final permission state in UI matches server state
No JS errors on side panel page
npx playwright test e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts (NEW file)

Context:
This is the most realistic chaos scenario: Claude is calling tools via MCP while the user is changing settings in the side panel. The permission change propagates through background script → MCP server → config.json. The in-flight tool call may or may not be affected depending on timing.

Changes:
Setup: standard server (OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS='') + extension + test server + matching tab. Open side panel. Create MCP client, initialize. Expand the e2e-test plugin card in side panel.

Start slow_with_progress (durationMs=3000) via mcpClient (don't await). While it's running, toggle the echo tool permission Off→Auto via selectPermission in the side panel. Wait for the slow call to settle (Promise.allSettled). Verify the side panel's permission select shows 'Auto'. Verify mcpClient.listTools() shows echo tool enabled. Make a fresh echo call to verify system is healthy. Collect page errors from side panel and assert zero.

Constraints:
- Need both side panel page AND MCP client connected to same server
- selectPermission is a Playwright helper from helpers.js
- The slow tool is NOT the echo tool — so permission change on echo shouldn't affect the slow call
- Mark plugin as reviewed in config to avoid unreviewed dialog

userStories.title: Multiple MCP sessions calling tools concurrently
userStories.description: Create 3 MCP client sessions, all calling tools in parallel, verify each gets correct responses with no cross-session contamination.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates 3 independent MCP clients (separate sessions) to the same server
Each client fires 5 echo calls with unique prefixed messages concurrently
All 15 calls succeed
Each client's results contain only its own prefixed messages (no cross-contamination)
Session IDs are different for each client
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts (add to same file)

Context:
In production, multiple AI agents (e.g., multiple Claude sessions) may connect to the same MCP server simultaneously. Each gets its own session via Streamable HTTP. Response routing must be session-scoped.

Changes:
Setup: standard server + extension + test server + matching tab. Create 3 MCP clients: client1, client2, client3. Initialize all 3 (each gets unique sessionId). Fire in parallel:
- client1: 5 echo calls with message 'c1-0' through 'c1-4'
- client2: 5 echo calls with message 'c2-0' through 'c2-4'
- client3: 5 echo calls with message 'c3-0' through 'c3-4'

All 15 via Promise.all. Verify all succeed. For each client, parse its 5 results and verify all messages start with the correct prefix ('c1-', 'c2-', 'c3-'). Verify sessionIds are all different.

Constraints:
- createMcpClient returns a new client each time — each has independent session state
- All 3 clients point to same port/secret
- The echo tool returns { message: <input> } — parse JSON to verify

userStories.title: CLI config change during active MCP dispatch
userStories.description: Simulate CLI modifying config.json (adding/removing plugin) while MCP client has active tool calls, verify no crash and eventual consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Test starts 3 slow tool calls via MCP client
Writes config.json removing the plugin (simulating CLI 'opentabs config' command)
Sends POST /reload (simulating CLI notification to server)
In-flight calls resolve (success or clean error)
After config restored, new tool calls succeed
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts (add to same file)

Context:
A user might run `opentabs plugin remove <name>` from the CLI while Claude is actively using that plugin's tools. The CLI modifies config.json and sends POST /reload. The server must handle the plugin disappearing mid-dispatch gracefully.

Changes:
Setup: standard server + extension + test server + matching tab. Start 3 slow_with_progress calls (durationMs=5000). After 500ms, writeTestConfig WITHOUT the e2e-test plugin (empty localPlugins). POST /reload. Let the slow calls settle via Promise.allSettled. Some/all will fail (plugin gone). Verify server health ok. Write config back with plugin. POST /reload. Wait for extension to reconnect + tab ready. Verify fresh echo call succeeds.

Constraints:
- POST /reload: fetch POST to http://localhost:${port}/reload with auth header
- The slow calls may fail with 'plugin not found' or similar — that's expected
- After restoring config, must wait for extension to re-inject adapter

userStories.title: Side panel search while MCP client installs plugin
userStories.description: Side panel user is searching for plugins while MCP client triggers a plugin install via config change, verify the search results and plugin list both update correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test has side panel open with search active (typing a query)
Config is modified to add a new plugin + POST /reload sent
Side panel plugin list updates to show the new plugin (via plugins.changed notification)
Search results clear when user clears search, new plugin is visible
No JS errors on side panel
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts (add to same file)

Context:
The side panel receives plugins.changed notifications when the server's plugin list changes. If the user is mid-search, the notification should update the underlying plugin list but the search filter should still work correctly.

Changes:
Setup: start server with NO plugins. Open side panel, verify empty/minimal state. Type 'test' in the search input. While search is active, writeTestConfig adding e2e-test plugin + POST /reload. Wait for side panel to receive the update (e2e-test should appear in search results since 'test' matches). Clear search. Verify E2E Test plugin card is visible in the full list.

Constraints:
- Start with no plugins so the addition is clearly observable
- The search input is `sp.locator('input[type="text"]').first()`
- plugins.changed notification triggers getFullState() refresh in side panel

userStories.title: Full system stress: 3 MCP clients + side panel + config changes simultaneously
userStories.description: The ultimate chaos test: 3 MCP clients making tool calls, side panel toggling permissions and theme, config being modified via file writes — all at the same time. Verify the system survives.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 MCP clients are making concurrent echo calls
Side panel is toggling theme and expanding/collapsing cards
Config is written to add/remove a plugin
After 10 seconds of chaos, all MCP clients can still make successful calls
Side panel shows correct final state (no stale data, no crash)
Server health returns ok
Zero JS errors on side panel
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts (add to same file)

Context:
This is the ultimate integration stress test. In production, multiple Claude sessions, a user on the side panel, and CLI commands can all be active simultaneously. The system must not deadlock, crash, or corrupt state.

Changes:
Setup: server (OPENTABS_DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS='') + extension + test server + matching tab. Open side panel. Create 3 MCP clients. Mark plugin as reviewed.

Run all of these concurrently for ~5 seconds:
1. Client 1: loop calling echo 10 times with 500ms between
2. Client 2: loop calling echo 10 times with 500ms between
3. Client 3: loop calling echo 10 times with 500ms between
4. Side panel: toggle theme 5 times, expand/collapse cards 5 times
5. Config: write config without plugin, POST /reload, wait 1s, write config with plugin, POST /reload

Use Promise.allSettled to run all 5 concurrent streams. After all settle:
- Verify server health ok
- Verify at least ONE of the 3 clients can make a successful echo call (system recovered)
- Verify side panel page has zero pageerror events
- Verify side panel shows the e2e-test plugin (config was restored)

Constraints:
- Use Promise.allSettled for the outer orchestration (some streams WILL have failures during config removal)
- The config removal will cause tool call failures — that's expected and correct behavior
- After config restoration + /reload, the system must recover
- Keep theme toggle and accordion interactions simple (just click, no selectPermission that requires Radix dropdown)
- Mark plugin as reviewed in initial config to avoid unreviewed dialog blocking
